100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On March 19, 1926, the British Empire faced rising tensions as a general strike loomed in South Wales, driven by coal miners demanding better wages amid economic hardship. In the United States, the Senate debated the future of Prohibition, with lawmakers divided over its impact on crime and public morality. Meanwhile, in Germany, political instability continued as the Weimar Republic struggled to maintain order amid economic strife and extremist pressures.
